name: Rollout Management
description: Manage deployment rollouts including monitoring progress, pausing, resuming, and rolling back to previous versions.
trendingReason: Zero-downtime deployments are essential for production reliability

steps.title: Check rollout status
steps.description: Monitor the progress of a deployment rollout.

steps.title: View rollout history
steps.description: See previous revisions of a deployment including change causes.
steps.tip: Add --revision=N to see the details of a specific revision.

steps.title: Pause a rollout
steps.description: Pause a rollout mid-way to inspect or run canary tests before proceeding.

steps.title: Resume a paused rollout
steps.description: Continue a paused rollout after validation.

steps.title: Rollback to the previous revision
steps.description: Revert a deployment to its last known good state.
steps.tip: Use --to-revision=N to roll back to a specific revision number from the history.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Rollout Management
set -euo pipefail

DEPLOYMENT=${1:?"Usage: $0 <deployment-name> [namespace]"}
NS=${2:-"default"}

echo "=== Deployment Status ==="
kubectl get deployment "$DEPLOYMENT" -n "$NS" -o wide

echo ""
echo "=== Rollout Status ==="
kubectl rollout status deployment/"$DEPLOYMENT" -n "$NS" --timeout=5s 2>&1 || true

echo ""
echo "=== Rollout History ==="
kubectl rollout history deployment/"$DEPLOYMENT" -n "$NS"

echo ""
echo "=== Current ReplicaSets ==="
kubectl get rs -n "$NS" -l "app=$DEPLOYMENT" -o wide 2>/dev/null || kubectl get rs -n "$NS" | head -10

echo ""
echo "=== Rollout Commands ==="
echo "Rollback:  kubectl rollout undo deployment/$DEPLOYMENT -n $NS"
echo "Pause:     kubectl rollout pause deployment/$DEPLOYMENT -n $NS"
echo "Resume:    kubectl rollout resume deployment/$DEPLOYMENT -n $NS"
echo "Revision:  kubectl rollout undo deployment/$DEPLOYMENT -n $NS --to-revision=N"

faq.question: How do I set a change cause for rollout history?
faq.answer: Use the annotation: 'kubectl annotate deployment/<name> kubernetes.io/change-cause="Updated image to v2.0"'. This appears in rollout history.

faq.question: What rollout strategy should I use?
faq.answer: RollingUpdate (default) replaces pods gradually. Recreate kills all old pods first. Use RollingUpdate for zero-downtime and Recreate when you cannot run two versions simultaneously.

faq.question: How many revisions are kept?
faq.answer: Controlled by .spec.revisionHistoryLimit, which defaults to 10. Old ReplicaSets beyond this limit are garbage collected.
